ashers census project

2026-03-23

Overview

Notes: -change census api key install = to TRUE if you want to install my API key. or use your own idk if it matters -the geography csv just provides a list of counties -right now it’s looking at plant annual CO2 emissions (plco2an) summed to a county level -you can’t have the excel file open while the program is looking at it (workbooks live in the egrid files folder)

  1. Setup
library(tidycensus)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tigris)
library(readxl)
library(janitor)
library(spdep)
library(ggplot2)

options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

# CONFIGURE GEOGRAPHY HERE
# Options: "arc" (Appalachian Regional Commission), 
#          "msa" (Metropolitan Statistical Areas), 
#          "state" (individual states), etc.
#geography_type <- "subregions"
geography_file <- "geography.csv"  # File with county list for selected geography

# CONFIGURE SUBREGION HERE
# Change this to analyze different subregions
#target_subregion <- "Central"

# CONFIGURE ANALYSIS YEAR HERE
# Change this to analyze different years (must be available in both ACS and eGRID)
analysis_year <- 2022

# Baseline year for change analysis (end year is analysis_year)
egrid_change_baseline_year <- 2012

# ENERGY VARIABLES (all three run in one knit)
#  - "renewable_mwh": change in renewable generation (MWh) (plgenacn + plgenabm)
#  - "co2_tons":      change in CO2 emissions (tons) (plco2an)
#  - "COAL":          change in generation from coal (MWh) (plgenacl)
energy_vars <- c("renewable_mwh", "co2_tons", "COAL")

energy_label_for <- function(energy_var) {
  if (energy_var == "renewable_mwh") {
    "Change in generation from renewables (MWh)"
  } else if (energy_var == "co2_tons") {
    "Change in annual CO2 emissions (tons)"
  } else {
    "Change in generation from coal (MWh)"
  }
}
  1. Load Geography Data We load selected geography’s county list and filter by subregion.
geography_df <- read_csv(geography_file) |>
  clean_names() |>
#  filter(subregion == target_subregion) |>
  mutate(fips = str_pad(fips, 5, pad = "0"))
## Rows: 423 Columns: 4
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (4): FIPS, COUNTY, STATE, SUBREGION
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
  1. Income Data (ACS) We use median household income (B19013_001). Why two versions? Aligned (analysis_year): Matches eGRID data → best for correlation Lagged (analysis_year-2): Income precedes energy → tests robustness

ACS pulls are stored in census_cache so re-running chunks in the same R session does not repeat API calls (clear census_cache or restart R if you change analysis_year, egrid_change_baseline_year, or other ACS settings).

Metro/micro CBSA boundaries for section 9 are also fetched here.

census_api_key("0b99bc09077560a4ae65416a235a4f0910a40637", install = FALSE)
## To install your API key for use in future sessions, run this function with `install = TRUE`.
if (!exists("census_cache", envir = .GlobalEnv)) {
  assign("census_cache", new.env(parent = emptyenv()), envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

if (!exists("income_aligned", envir = census_cache, inherits = FALSE)) {
  income_analysis <- get_acs(
    geography = "county",
    variables = "B19013_001",
    year = analysis_year,
    survey = "acs5"
  ) |>
    select(GEOID, estimate) |>
    rename(median_income = estimate)

  income_baseline <- get_acs(
    geography = "county",
    variables = "B19013_001",
    year = egrid_change_baseline_year,
    survey = "acs5"
  ) |>
    select(GEOID, estimate) |>
    rename(median_income_baseline = estimate)

  assign(
    "income_aligned",
    income_analysis |>
      left_join(income_baseline, by = "GEOID") |>
      mutate(median_income_change = median_income - median_income_baseline),
    envir = census_cache
  )
}
## Getting data from the 2018-2022 5-year ACS
## Getting data from the 2008-2012 5-year ACS
if (!exists("income_lagged", envir = census_cache, inherits = FALSE)) {
  assign(
    "income_lagged",
    get_acs(
      geography = "county",
      variables = "B19013_001",
      year = analysis_year - 2,
      survey = "acs5"
    ) |>
      select(GEOID, estimate) |>
      rename(median_income = estimate),
    envir = census_cache
  )
}
## Getting data from the 2016-2020 5-year ACS
if (!exists("cbsa_sf", envir = census_cache, inherits = FALSE)) {
  assign(
    "cbsa_sf",
    get_acs(
      geography = "metropolitan statistical area/micropolitan statistical area",
      variables = "B01001_001",
      year = analysis_year,
      survey = "acs5",
      geometry = TRUE
    ) |>
      st_transform(crs = 5070) |>
      mutate(
        cbsa_type = case_when(
          str_detect(NAME, "Metro Area") ~ "Metropolitan",
          str_detect(NAME, "Micro Area") ~ "Micropolitan",
          TRUE ~ NA_character_
        )
      ) |>
      select(cbsa_type),
    envir = census_cache
  )
}
## Getting data from the 2018-2022 5-year ACS
income_aligned <- get("income_aligned", envir = census_cache)
income_lagged <- get("income_lagged", envir = census_cache)
cbsa_sf <- get("cbsa_sf", envir = census_cache)

# Restrict to selected geography
income_aligned_app <- income_aligned |>
  inner_join(geography_df, by = c("GEOID" = "fips"))

income_lagged_app <- income_lagged |>
  inner_join(geography_df, by = c("GEOID" = "fips"))
  1. eGRID Data (Energy Change) We load plant-level eGRID data for the baseline and analysis years, aggregate to county, and compute change for each entry in energy_vars. Workbooks live in egrid files/ as eGRID{year}_data.xlsx. The plant sheet is either PLNT{yy} or EGRDPLNT{yy} (yy = last two digits of the year).
load_egrid_year <- function(analysis_year, baseline_year, energy_var = c("renewable_mwh", "co2_tons", "COAL")) {
  energy_var <- match.arg(energy_var)

  # Read one year, normalize county FIPS, and aggregate selected energy metric.
  load_plants_for_year <- function(year, energy_var) {
    file_name <- file.path("egrid files", paste0("eGRID", year, "_data.xlsx"))
    sheets <- excel_sheets(file_name)
    yy <- sprintf("%02d", year %% 100)
    sheet_plnt <- paste0("PLNT", yy)
    sheet_egrd <- paste0("EGRDPLNT", yy)
    sheet_name <- if (sheet_plnt %in% sheets) {
      sheet_plnt
    } else if (sheet_egrd %in% sheets) {
      sheet_egrd
    } else {
      stop(
        "No plant sheet ", sheet_plnt, " or ", sheet_egrd, " in ", file_name,
        ". Available: ", paste(sheets, collapse = ", ")
      )
    }

    plants <- read_excel(file_name, sheet = sheet_name, skip = 1) |>
      clean_names() |>
      filter(!is.na(fipsst), !is.na(fipscnty)) |>
      mutate(
        fips = str_c(str_pad(fipsst, 2, pad = "0"), str_pad(fipscnty, 3, pad = "0"))
      )

    if (energy_var == "renewable_mwh") {
      plants <- plants |>
        mutate(energy_value = plgenacn + plgenabm)
    } else if (energy_var == "co2_tons") {
      plants <- plants |>
        mutate(energy_value = plco2an)
    } else if (energy_var == "COAL") {
      plants <- plants |>
        mutate(energy_value = plgenacl)
    }

    plants |>
      filter(!is.na(energy_value)) |>
      group_by(fips) |>
      summarise(energy = sum(energy_value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  }

  county_baseline <- load_plants_for_year(baseline_year, energy_var) |>
    rename(energy_baseline = energy)

  county_analysis <- load_plants_for_year(analysis_year, energy_var) |>
    rename(energy_end = energy)

  county_change <- county_baseline |>
    inner_join(county_analysis, by = "fips") |>
    mutate(energy_change = energy_end - energy_baseline)

  county_change
}

county_energy_by_var <- energy_vars |>
  set_names() |>
  map(function(ev) load_egrid_year(analysis_year, egrid_change_baseline_year, ev))

## match EIA data. The eGRID sequence numbers match those of Brush 1&3, ORISPL:

## capacity. The plant name was updated to Brush Generation Facility to match EIA
## data.'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK7183 / R7183C141: got 'Other'
# County-year panel across all available eGRID years in analysis period.
load_egrid_panel <- function(baseline_year, analysis_year, energy_var = c("renewable_mwh", "co2_tons", "COAL")) {
  energy_var <- match.arg(energy_var)

  egrid_files <- list.files(
    path = "egrid files",
    pattern = "^eGRID[0-9]{4}_data\\.xlsx$",
    full.names = FALSE
  )

  available_years <- egrid_files |>
    str_extract("[0-9]{4}") |>
    as.integer() |>
    discard(is.na) |>
    sort()

  years_in_period <- available_years[available_years >= baseline_year & available_years <= analysis_year]

  if (length(years_in_period) == 0) {
    stop(
      "No eGRID files found for years between ", baseline_year, " and ", analysis_year, "."
    )
  }

  map_dfr(years_in_period, function(y) {
    load_egrid_year(y, y, energy_var) |>
      transmute(fips, year = y, e_var = energy_end)
  })
}
  1. Build Analysis Datasets For each energy variable, we use the corresponding county_energy (with energy_change) as the base dataset, then join income data.
analysis_county_energy_by_var <- county_energy_by_var |>
  map(function(county_energy) {
    county_energy |>
      inner_join(
        income_aligned_app |>
          select(GEOID, median_income, median_income_change) |>
          rename(median_income_aligned = median_income),
        by = c("fips" = "GEOID")
      ) |>
      left_join(
        income_lagged_app |>
          select(GEOID, median_income) |>
          rename(median_income_lagged = median_income),
        by = c("fips" = "GEOID")
      )
  })

analysis_energy_change_by_var <- analysis_county_energy_by_var |>
  map(~ drop_na(.x, median_income_aligned, energy_change))
  1. Change in energy variable vs median household income For each entry in energy_vars, we take that eGRID metric at egrid_change_baseline_year and at analysis_year, compute county-level change inside load_egrid_year(), and work with the resulting energy_change column.
walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  analysis_energy_change <- analysis_energy_change_by_var[[ev]]
  cat("\n============================\n")
  cat("CHANGE ANALYSIS [", ev, "]: (", sep = "")
  cat(
    egrid_change_baseline_year,
    " → ",
    analysis_year,
    ") vs median household income (ACS ",
    analysis_year,
    ", B19013_001)\n",
    sep = ""
  )
  cat("============================\n")

  cor_change <- cor.test(
    analysis_energy_change$median_income_aligned,
    analysis_energy_change$energy_change,
    method = "pearson"
  )
  print(cor_change)

  model_change <- lm(energy_change ~ median_income_aligned, data = analysis_energy_change)
  print(summary(model_change))
})
## 
## ============================
## CHANGE ANALYSIS [renewable_mwh]: (2012 → 2022) vs median household income (ACS 2022, B19013_001)
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_energy_change$median_income_aligned and analysis_energy_change$energy_change
## t = 0.68812, df = 192, p-value = 0.4922
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.09191744  0.18915282
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## 0.04959966 
## 
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = energy_change ~ median_income_aligned, data = analysis_energy_change)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -537007 -150521 -117603  -55727 8211830 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                         Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)           -50844.883 276103.196  -0.184    0.854
## median_income_aligned      3.199      4.649   0.688    0.492
## 
## Residual standard error: 702100 on 192 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.00246,    Adjusted R-squared:  -0.002735 
## F-statistic: 0.4735 on 1 and 192 DF,  p-value: 0.4922
## 
## 
## ============================
## CHANGE ANALYSIS [co2_tons]: (2012 → 2022) vs median household income (ACS 2022, B19013_001)
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_energy_change$median_income_aligned and analysis_energy_change$energy_change
## t = -2.2897, df = 125, p-value = 0.02371
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.3621892 -0.0273766
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## -0.2006343 
## 
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = energy_change ~ median_income_aligned, data = analysis_energy_change)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -16787091   -194145    438894    894397   4535574 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                         Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)           2744346.71 1452003.11    1.89   0.0611 .
## median_income_aligned     -56.12      24.51   -2.29   0.0237 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2623000 on 125 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.04025,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.03258 
## F-statistic: 5.243 on 1 and 125 DF,  p-value: 0.02371
## 
## 
## ============================
## CHANGE ANALYSIS [COAL]: (2012 → 2022) vs median household income (ACS 2022, B19013_001)
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_energy_change$median_income_aligned and analysis_energy_change$energy_change
## t = -2.1122, df = 192, p-value = 0.03596
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.28551070 -0.01003529
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## -0.1506971 
## 
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = energy_change ~ median_income_aligned, data = analysis_energy_change)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -17832549    287454    543467    746835   4365235 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                         Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)           1121178.64  870737.45   1.288    0.199  
## median_income_aligned     -30.97      14.66  -2.112    0.036 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 2214000 on 192 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.02271,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.01762 
## F-statistic: 4.462 on 1 and 192 DF,  p-value: 0.03596
walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  analysis_energy_change <- analysis_energy_change_by_var[[ev]]
  print(
    ggplot(analysis_energy_change, aes(x = median_income_aligned, y = energy_change)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(
          "[",
          ev,
          "] Change in energy variable (",
          egrid_change_baseline_year,
          "–",
          analysis_year,
          ") vs median household income (",
          analysis_year,
          " ACS)"
        ),
        x = "Median household income (B19013_001)",
        y = energy_label_for(ev)
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
})
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

6b. Change in energy variable vs change in median household income For each energy variable, we correlate county-level energy_change with county-level median_income_change (analysis year minus baseline year income).

walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  analysis_income_change <- analysis_county_energy_by_var[[ev]] |>
    drop_na(median_income_change, energy_change)

  cat("\n============================\n")
  cat("INCOME-CHANGE CORRELATION [", ev, "]\n", sep = "")
  cat("============================\n")

  cor_income_change <- cor.test(
    analysis_income_change$median_income_change,
    analysis_income_change$energy_change,
    method = "pearson"
  )
  print(cor_income_change)
})
## 
## ============================
## INCOME-CHANGE CORRELATION [renewable_mwh]
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_income_change$median_income_change and analysis_income_change$energy_change
## t = 1.6998, df = 192, p-value = 0.09078
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.01944536  0.25820873
## sample estimates:
##      cor 
## 0.121763 
## 
## 
## ============================
## INCOME-CHANGE CORRELATION [co2_tons]
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_income_change$median_income_change and analysis_income_change$energy_change
## t = -1.5334, df = 125, p-value = 0.1277
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.30292215  0.03926586
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## -0.1358783 
## 
## 
## ============================
## INCOME-CHANGE CORRELATION [COAL]
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_income_change$median_income_change and analysis_income_change$energy_change
## t = -1.2172, df = 192, p-value = 0.225
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.22560081  0.05403387
## sample estimates:
##         cor 
## -0.08750707
walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  analysis_income_change <- analysis_county_energy_by_var[[ev]] |>
    drop_na(median_income_change, energy_change)

  print(
    ggplot(analysis_income_change, aes(x = median_income_change, y = energy_change)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(
          "[",
          ev,
          "] Change in energy variable (",
          egrid_change_baseline_year,
          "–",
          analysis_year,
          ") vs change in median household income"
        ),
        x = paste0(
          "Change in median household income (ACS ",
          egrid_change_baseline_year,
          " to ",
          analysis_year,
          ")"
        ),
        y = energy_label_for(ev)
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
})
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

  1. Choropleth: change in selected energy variable Fill is energy_change from analysis_energy_change_by_var[[ev]] for each ev in energy_vars. A light basemap of all counties in the selected states is drawn underneath for surrounding context.
state_fips <- geography_df |>
  mutate(state_fips = str_sub(fips, 1, 2)) |>
  distinct(state_fips) |>
  pull(state_fips)

counties_sf <- counties(state = state_fips, cb = TRUE, year = 2020) |>
  st_transform(crs = 5070)

counties_map_change_by_var <- analysis_energy_change_by_var |>
  map(~ counties_sf |>
    inner_join(
      .x |>
        select(fips, energy_change),
      by = c("GEOID" = "fips")
    ))
walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  counties_map_change <- counties_map_change_by_var[[ev]]
  print(
    ggplot() +
      geom_sf(data = counties_sf, fill = "grey95", color = "grey80", linewidth = 0.05) +
      geom_sf(data = counties_map_change, aes(fill = energy_change), color = "grey35", linewidth = 0.08) +
      scale_fill_gradient2(
        low = "#3B8132",
        mid = "#f7f7f7",
        high = "#7F2B0A",
        midpoint = 0,
        name = "Change in\nenergy variable",
        labels = scales::label_comma()
      ) +
      theme_void() +
      theme(legend.position = "right") +
      labs(
        title = paste0(
          "[",
          ev,
          "] Change in energy variable, ",
          egrid_change_baseline_year,
          "–",
          analysis_year
        ),
        subtitle = "Colored counties are analysis counties; grey counties provide surrounding-area context",
        caption = paste0("Variable: ", ev, "; fill uses energy_change.")
      )
  )
})

  1. Partisanship vs change in selected energy variable
# Read partisanship directly from CSV and keep 2022 county-level values.
partisan_join <- read_csv("partisanship.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  filter(YEAR == 2022) |>
  transmute(
    fips = str_pad(as.character(STCOFIPS10), 5, pad = "0"),
    PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM = as.numeric(PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM)
  )

if (nrow(partisan_join) == 0) {
  stop("No 2022 rows found in partisanship.csv.")
}

walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  analysis_energy_change <- analysis_energy_change_by_var[[ev]]
  analysis_partisanship <- analysis_energy_change |>
    left_join(partisan_join, by = "fips") |>
    drop_na(PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM, energy_change)

  cor_partisan <- cor.test(
    analysis_partisanship$PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM,
    analysis_partisanship$energy_change,
    method = "pearson"
  )

  cat("\n============================\n")
  cat("PARTISANSHIP CORRELATION [", ev, "]\n", sep = "")
  cat("============================\n")
  print(cor_partisan)

  print(
    ggplot(
      analysis_partisanship,
      aes(x = PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM, y = energy_change)
    ) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.35) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
      scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(0, 1),
        breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2),
        labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.1)
      ) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(
          "[",
          ev,
          "] Partisan index vs change in energy variable (",
          egrid_change_baseline_year,
          "–",
          analysis_year,
          ")"
        ),
        x = "PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM (0 to 1)",
        y = energy_label_for(ev)
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
})
## 
## ============================
## PARTISANSHIP CORRELATION [renewable_mwh]
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_partisanship$PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM and analysis_partisanship$energy_change
## t = -0.92315, df = 192, p-value = 0.3571
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.20542660  0.07510263
## sample estimates:
##         cor 
## -0.06647553
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## 
## ============================
## PARTISANSHIP CORRELATION [co2_tons]
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_partisanship$PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM and analysis_partisanship$energy_change
## t = 0.64506, df = 125, p-value = 0.5201
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.1177962  0.2295120
## sample estimates:
##        cor 
## 0.05760049
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

## 
## ============================
## PARTISANSHIP CORRELATION [COAL]
## ============================
## 
##  Pearson's product-moment correlation
## 
## data:  analysis_partisanship$PARTISAN_INDEX_DEM and analysis_partisanship$energy_change
## t = -0.073088, df = 192, p-value = 0.9418
## alternative hypothesis: true correlation is not equal to 0
## 95 percent confidence interval:
##  -0.1460409  0.1357011
## sample estimates:
##          cor 
## -0.005274604
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

  1. Metro vs micro vs rural (Census CBSA delineations)
# CBSA polygons from ACS (loaded once in section 3, cached in census_cache)

# Classify counties by whether their interior point lies in a metro/micro CBSA polygon
county_cbsa <- counties_sf |>
  st_point_on_surface() |>
  st_join(cbsa_sf, join = st_within, left = TRUE) |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  transmute(
    fips = GEOID,
    cbsa_type = replace_na(cbsa_type, "Rural")
  )
## Warning: st_point_on_surface assumes attributes are constant over geometries
walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  analysis_energy_change <- analysis_energy_change_by_var[[ev]]
  analysis_cbsa <- analysis_energy_change |>
    left_join(county_cbsa, by = "fips") |>
    mutate(
      county_type = cbsa_type,
      county_type = factor(county_type, levels = c("Metropolitan", "Micropolitan", "Rural"))
    )

  cbsa_summary <- analysis_cbsa |>
    group_by(county_type) |>
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean_change = mean(energy_change, na.rm = TRUE),
      sd_change = sd(energy_change, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  print(
    ggplot(cbsa_summary, aes(x = county_type, y = mean_change, fill = county_type)) +
      geom_col(width = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
      geom_errorbar(
        aes(ymin = mean_change - sd_change, ymax = mean_change + sd_change),
        width = 0.18,
        linewidth = 0.6
      ) +
      scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Metropolitan" = "#E63400", "Micropolitan" = "#FF8B0F", "Rural" = "#6C3400")) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(
          "[",
          ev,
          "] Mean change in energy variable by county CBSA type, ",
          egrid_change_baseline_year,
          "–",
          analysis_year
        ),
        subtitle = "Error bars show +/- 1 standard deviation",
        x = NULL,
        y = energy_label_for(ev),
        caption = "CBSA classification from ACS metro/micro geographies via tidycensus."
      ) +
      theme_minimal()
  )
})

  1. ANOVA: annual energy variable by year/state/subregion/CBSA
anova_df_by_var <- energy_vars |>
  set_names() |>
  map(function(ev) {
    egrid_panel <- load_egrid_panel(
      baseline_year = egrid_change_baseline_year,
      analysis_year = analysis_year,
      energy_var = ev
    )

    egrid_panel |>
      inner_join(
        geography_df |> select(fips, state, subregion),
        by = "fips"
      ) |>
      left_join(
        county_cbsa |>
          transmute(
            fips,
            cbsa_factor = case_when(
              cbsa_type == "Metropolitan" ~ "Metropolitan",
              cbsa_type == "Micropolitan" ~ "Micropolitan",
              TRUE ~ "Rural/Neither"
            )
          ),
        by = "fips"
      ) |>
      mutate(
        year = factor(year),
        state = factor(state),
        subregion = factor(subregion),
        cbsa_factor = factor(cbsa_factor, levels = c("Metropolitan", "Micropolitan", "Rural/Neither"))
      ) |>
      drop_na(e_var, year, state, subregion, cbsa_factor)
  })
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK2240 / R2240C141: got 'Spencer City of'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EN2240 / R2240C144: got 'Webster City City of'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK2366 / R2366C141: got 'Municipal Energy Agency
## of NE'
## Warning: Expecting logical in FK2863 / R2863C167: got 'Wabash Valley Power IGCC
## (ORISPL: 57482) was combined into Wabash River (ORISPL: 1010) to match EIA
## data. The eGRID sequence numbers match those of Wabash River, ORISPL: 1010, in
## past versions of eGRID. The ownership data was combined and the percentage of
## ownership for each owner was recalculated using nameplate capacity.'

## data. The eGRID sequence numbers match those of Wabash River, ORISPL: 1010, in
## past versions of eGRID. The ownership data was combined and the percentage of
## ownership for each owner was recalculated using nameplate capacity.'
## Warning: Expecting logical in FK3132 / R3132C167: got 'Nelson Industrial Steam
## Company (ORISPL: 50030) was combined into R S Nelson (ORISPL: 1393) to match
## EIA data. The eGRID sequence numbers match those of R S Nelson, ORISPL: 1393,
## in past versions of eGRID. The ownership data was combined and the percentage
## of ownership for each owner was recalculated using nameplate capacity.'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK4881 / R4881C141: got 'MSR Public Power Agency'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EN4881 / R4881C144: got 'Tucson Electric Power
## Co'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EQ4881 / R4881C147: got 'Tri-State G & T Assn
## Inc'
## Warning: Expecting logical in ET4881 / R4881C150: got 'Utah Associated Mun
## Power Sys'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5384 / R5384C141: got 'Other'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5387 / R5387C141: got 'Other'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5402 / R5402C141: got 'Other'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5420 / R5420C141: got 'Other'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5435 / R5435C141: got 'Other'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5474 / R5474C141: got 'Other'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5480 / R5480C141: got 'Wadsworth City of'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EN5480 / R5480C144: got 'Other'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK5767 / R5767C141: got 'Duquesne Conemaugh LLC'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EN5767 / R5767C144: got 'Conemaugh Power LLC'
## Warning: Expecting logical in FK6447 / R6447C167: got 'Brush 2 (ORISPL: 10683)
## and Brush 4 (ORISPL: 55209) were combined into Brush 1&3 (ORISPL: 10682) to
## match EIA data. The eGRID sequence numbers match those of Brush 1&3, ORISPL:
## 10682, in past versions of eGRID. The ownership data was combined and the
## percentage of ownership for each owner was recalculated using nameplate
## capacity. The plant name was updated to Brush Generation Facility to match EIA
## data.'
## Warning: Expecting logical in EK7183 / R7183C141: got 'Other'
walk(energy_vars, function(ev) {
  anova_df <- anova_df_by_var[[ev]]
  anova_model <- lm(e_var ~ year + state + subregion + cbsa_factor, data = anova_df)
  anova_results <- anova(anova_model)

  cat("\n============================\n")
  cat("ANOVA [", ev, "]: e_var ~ year + state + subregion + cbsa_factor\n", sep = "")
  cat("============================\n")
  print(anova_results)
})
## 
## ============================
## ANOVA [renewable_mwh]: e_var ~ year + state + subregion + cbsa_factor
## ============================
## Analysis of Variance Table
## 
## Response: e_var
##              Df     Sum Sq    Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
## year          3 3.2683e+12 1.0894e+12  0.1020    0.9588    
## state        12 5.3157e+14 4.4297e+13  4.1492 2.443e-06 ***
## subregion     3 1.6833e+13 5.6110e+12  0.5256    0.6648    
## cbsa_factor   2 1.6155e+13 8.0777e+12  0.7566    0.4696    
## Residuals   808 8.6262e+15 1.0676e+13                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## ============================
## ANOVA [co2_tons]: e_var ~ year + state + subregion + cbsa_factor
## ============================
## Analysis of Variance Table
## 
## Response: e_var
##              Df     Sum Sq    Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
## year          3 8.0308e+12 2.6769e+12  0.1695  0.916946    
## state        12 1.1580e+15 9.6503e+13  6.1122 3.138e-10 ***
## subregion     3 9.7000e+13 3.2333e+13  2.0479  0.105932    
## cbsa_factor   2 1.8459e+14 9.2296e+13  5.8457  0.003045 ** 
## Residuals   655 1.0342e+16 1.5789e+13                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## ============================
## ANOVA [COAL]: e_var ~ year + state + subregion + cbsa_factor
## ============================
## Analysis of Variance Table
## 
## Response: e_var
##              Df     Sum Sq    Mean Sq F value    Pr(>F)    
## year          3 1.4737e+14 4.9125e+13  4.2301  0.005588 ** 
## state        12 7.7208e+14 6.4340e+13  5.5402 3.701e-09 ***
## subregion     3 4.5722e+13 1.5241e+13  1.3124  0.269130    
## cbsa_factor   2 1.1871e+14 5.9355e+13  5.1110  0.006227 ** 
## Residuals   808 9.3835e+15 1.1613e+13                      
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
